evals.prompt: Plan only: turn "add rate limiting to the public API" into a work plan for acme/api-server. Don't implement anything yet.
evals.expected_output: Resolves scope and auth interactively, launches the bundled workflow script exactly once with mode plan (grounding + epic-decomposition + board placement + milestone + build order happen inside it), reports the hierarchy and branch-protection findings, surfaces any deferred questions, and stops — no execute launch, no code, no PR.

deterministic_checks.description: Orchestration is launched from the bundled workflow script, not improvised inline
Plan-only run launches the workflow in plan mode (matches both JSON tool-call args and JS-literal snippets)

deterministic_checks.command: python3 -c "import re,sys; t=open('transcript.md').read(); sys.exit(1 if re.search(r'[\"\\']?mode[\"\\']?\\s*:\\s*[\"\\']execute[\"\\']', t) else 0)"
deterministic_checks.description: Plan-only run never launches the execute mode, in either arg-literal format
Plan-only run never opens a PR at all

expectations: get_agent_capabilities auth preflight runs before the workflow launches
The reported hierarchy comes from the plan-mode workflow result (Epic + children + Kind/Status + milestone outcome + branch-protection findings)
Deferred items from the workflow result (no-milestone-fits, design-question offers) are surfaced to the user, not silently dropped
Stops after the plan report; no execute launch, no code, no PR

evals.prompt: Execute the plan for Epic #200 in acme/api-server: implement every Task, then open the PR.
evals.expected_output: Confirms the existing hierarchy via list_sub_issues (skipping a redundant plan launch), launches the workflow in execute mode with the build-order task list, and reports the result: sequential Tasks on one shared branch, one PR via create_pull_request, classified, boarded, linkage confirmed via get_linked_issues, settled verdict — and no merge.

deterministic_checks.description: Execution goes through the workflow's execute mode (matches both JSON tool-call args and JS-literal snippets)
PR-to-issue linkage is confirmed structurally, not assumed from PR body text
PR creation goes through create_pull_request, never the raw gh CLI
Nothing merges without the user's own approval turn

expectations: A seed with a full existing hierarchy skips the plan launch instead of re-decomposing
All Task implementations land on a single shared branch, sequentially in build order
One PR referencing every completed Task with Closes #N; classify_pull_request and add_pull_request_to_project both happen
The run ends at a settled/not-settled report — merging waits for explicit user approval in the trigger skill

evals.prompt: While implementing Task #205 the workflow's task agent notices the existing retry logic silently swallows a timeout error. File it and keep going.
evals.expected_output: The defect goes through the file-bug skill (with its own duplicate check) rather than a hand-rolled create_issue, gets lifecycle-managed via set_lifecycle_state if resolved in-run, and the Task line continues; the filed bug number appears in the workflow result's bugsFiled list.

deterministic_checks.description: Uses the file-bug skill for the discovered defect
file-bug's own duplicate check ran before a new issue was created

expectations: The discovered bug is filed through file-bug, not a standalone create_issue call
If resolved in the same run, its lifecycle state is advanced via set_lifecycle_state rather than left untouched
Task implementation continues after the bug is filed; the bug number is tracked in the final report's bugsFiled list

evals.prompt: PR #310 for Epic #200 just got merged. Close the loop.
evals.expected_output: Close-out runs in the trigger skill (not a workflow launch): confirms the merge is real, calls sync_linked_issues_project_field to stamp the post-merge board field across every linked issue, rolls Story/Epic Status to Done only after checking it isn't already there, and reports any skippedCrossRepo entries needing manual follow-up.

deterministic_checks.description: Uses the plugin's own post-merge field-sync tool
Checks current Status before writing Done rather than blindly overwriting it

expectations: sync_linked_issues_project_field is called after merge, never before
Any skippedCrossRepo issues are reported for manual follow-up, not silently dropped
Story/Epic Status rollup checks current state before writing rather than assuming it's still Todo/In Progress
